observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management assign a committee to conduct and
submit the physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant keep complete
subsidiary ledgers and make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial
Statements in strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the Inventories
Held for Distribution and for Consumption according to the correct quantity on
hand and its valuation as of year-end supported with inventory reports and
supplies ledger cards.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record Merchandise
Inventory (1-04-0-010) and Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers (1-04-04-
20) for the inventories of accountable forms for sale and for use in operations
costing P30,660.00 and P115,550.00, respectively; and henceforth record the
purchases of accountable forms to the pertinent inventory accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adjust Due to BIR to
the actual amounts of taxes withheld as of December 31, 2016 totaling
P775,051.76 and henceforth monitor taxes withheld and remitted.

We recommend that Management augment the personnel at the
Accounting Office to facilitate early submission of transaction documents and
financial reports.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget
Officer to prepare early the Consolidated Financial Statements and
Consolidated Status of Appropriations, Commitments and Balances (CSACB)
for CYs 2010-2015 to facilitate their prompt submission in compliance with the
provisions of the Accounting Systems Manual for Barangays.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the Prepaid
Insurance for the unexpired portion amounting to P444,278.32 and adjust the
account Insurance Expenses to a balance of P128,991.69 for the expired
portion of the insurance premiums.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor the
remittances by the barangay treasurers for the proceeds of CTCs.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit:
a. a justification why the Barangay Sto. Niño subproject was
reported as 100% completed despite the POW not having been
actually followed;
b. enforce with the contractor to do corrective repair and fast track
the completion of the subproject; and
c. refrain from approving the withdrawal of the retention fee, if
claimed by the contractor.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare
the monthly Report of Disbursements in compliance with Section 3e, Article
III of the MOA.

We recommend that Management immediately cause the return the
unutilized financial assistance to DOST amounting to P2,908,134.00 in
accordance with the provisions of the MOA, considering the lapse of four (4)
years from the time the financial assistance was received by the LGU.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. direct the concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, specifically
on the liquidation of cash advances;

b. refrain from granting cash advance if the previous cash advance is
not yet liquidated;

. institute stringent measures and policies to properly monitor the
grant and liquidation of cash advances to safeguard the
government funds from possible misuse; and

d. direct the City Accountant to enforce the withholding of salaries of
the accountable officers who failed to settle their cash advances on
time. Moreover, institute the appropriate administrative actions
against them, if warranted, for their failure to settle their
accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
derecognize the cost of vehicles already disposed of totaling £2,574,800.00,
and recognize the loss of P147,853.12 on the sale of the unserviceable
vehicles.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the General Services
Officer (GSO) to determine the cost of the vehicles based on Deeds of
Donation. Otherwise, determine their fair value to serve as basis of the City
Accountant to record in the books of the City all vehicles donated by other
government agencies or private entities to properly account all motor
vehicles owned and utilized by the City officials, and register the same in the
name of the City Government of Tanauan to establish rightful ownership by
the City of these donated properties.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to
make proper adjustments in the books to ensure that transactions relative to
the PCSO Charity Fund and DOST are properly accounted as income of the
City, and be made available for appropriation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. submit to the Provincial Accountant the copy of the Adjudication
and Settlement Board Decision No. 2007-045, writing off the
outstanding receivables of the City as this can be used as
supporting documents for their request to wnte-off the
corresponding receivable from the City; and

b. request for the release/remittance of the outstanding payables to
the City to rid the books of the Province of the long outstanding
unreconciled balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of the
Cooperatives and Livelihood Development Office to:

* exert more efforts in collecting from the delinquent borrowers by
going after the co-makers, if there are any;

* require in the loan agreement the co-makers and collateral so that
in case of non-payment, the City could go after them or have
something to recover from;

¢ determine the collectability of the doubtful loans and if found that
collection is no longer possible, coordinate with the Accounting
Office to request from the Commission on Audit the write-off of
those accounts by submitting all the required documents; and

¢ coordinate with the City Accountant for the immediate
reconciliation of receivables.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:

a. issue demand letter to TPMMPC thru its officers for the collection of
their outstanding balance as provided in the Memorandum of Agreement
(MOA), and if collected, remit immediately the amount to the source
agency, the Office of the President; and

b. verify the status of the Cooperative from the Cooperative Development
Authority (CDA) and if all the efforts are exerted to collect, the City may
submit all the documents evidencing the closure of the Cooperative and
collection efforts exerted by the City to the Office of the President to
inform them of the status, which may request for the write-off of the
receivables, and until such is approved, the City could not apply for the
writing-ofT of the receivables from the Cooperative.

We recommended that the City Mayor :

* direct the Office of the City Engineer to immediately prepare the
POW for the improvement of the TB DOTS RHU 11 building, and
follow-up from School Head of Poblacion I the location where the
school clinic could be constructed; and

¢ instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to remit to the
Bureau of the Treasury the unexpended balances of the national
funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City ENRO to
provide the data on the actual distance covered in all completed routes of
garbage collection for each billing period for verification of the performance
of the Contractor.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMO to
ensure that the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness as presented in the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the
projects and activities to be funded, including those planned for the
utilization of the previous year’'s LDRRMF as required under Items 5.1.2
and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to coordinate with other
concerned City offices such as Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and
Engineering Office for the proper and timely implementation of scheduled
projects, program and activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GFPS-TWG
Chairperson to appropriate the mandatory appropriation for GAD Fund,
and utilize intensively the HGDG checklist tool in order to effectively identify
and design gender responsive PPAs.

We also recommended the optimal utilization of the GAD fund by
strictly monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that the
intent of GAD in promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and
fulfilling women's human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment as
mandated could be fully achieved.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Local
Development Council to conduct feasibility of all the projects included in the
20% Development Fund to avoid delay in the implementation caused by
insufficient fund, site location or inaccurate program to maximize their
implementation and to ensure that the full intention of development would
benefit all the constituents, and carefully choose each project to be included
in the planned program.

‘We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO and the BAC
to consolidate all the requisitions for the quarter, and conduct public bidding
as the general mode of procurement to achieve fair competition and price most
advantageous to the government.

Also, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC to
adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 9184 on the alternative modes of
procurement like shopping and negotiated contract thru small value
procurement, to assure the City Government of a procurement process that is
proper and legitimate.

Likewise, require the Cooperatives to strictly comply with the
provision of Article 63.2 of RA No. 6938 or the Cooperative Code of the
Philippines.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly
adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and pertinent
provisions of RA No. 6713 and to refrain from procuring from the
Cooperatives unless after thorough evaluation of the submitted documents,
was determined to be an eligible bidder with lowest calculated bid.

We recommended that the City Mayor stop immediately the granting
of honoraria in excess of the provided rates to BAC assigned in the
procurement of goods and services and to members of BAC Secretariat
whose assignments are in the Procurement Unit of the City, the GSO, to
ensure compliance with Sections 5.3 and 5.6 of DBM Budget Circular No.
2004-5A dated October 7, 2005. Likewise, require the concerned employees
to refund the amount paid as honoraria in excess of limitations provided, and
in the performance of activities similar to the function for which they are
required to perform as employees assigned in the Procurement Unit, GSO, of
the City of Tanauan.

‘We recommended that the City Mayor stop approving the payment of
honoraria to the members of the Board of Trustees of TCC who are
government officials or employees unless sufficient legal basis for the grant of
honorarium has been submitted to the Audit Team for further evaluation,
otherwise, the payment of the said honoraria would be considered an audit
disallowance.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC - Schools
Division Superintendent to refrain from utilizing the SEF for expenditures
which do not conform to the allowable expenses under existing regulations.
Likewise, expenditures incurred in CY 2016 by the City Government,
contrary to Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and
DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-A and B shall not be allowed in audit.

We also recommended that the OIC - Schools Division and Assistant
Schools Division Superintendent submit an authority to travel on official
business from the DepEd Secretary for their travel to accompany the
participants in the 2016 World Pony Series Mustang 9 Championship,
otherwise, refund the amount of P127,000.00

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned officials
to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 with
regard to the submission of contracts to the Audit Team, and the PBAC and
other concerned official to comment/justify whether the changes in the dates
of the Bidding Process were properly communicated to the project proponent
through Bid Bulletins. Further, provide the lacking supporting documents
noted above, to help the Audit Team to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) direct the City Engineer to ensure that relevant information in all
infrastructure projects are posted in tarpaulin or similar materials
to inform the public of the ongoing projects of the City; and

b) remind other Department Heads of the posting to appropriate
signboards of the programs/activities for implementation by the
concerned department as required in COA Circular No. 2013-
004.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant
and City Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and
DBM NBC No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees
and annual dues to various leagues, and ensure that payments of such kind to
private organization shall not be charged against the government funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the PBAC and other
concerned City officials to provide the Audit Team of the bases of the
P500,000.00 minimum fixed monthly revenue share of the City as stipulated
in the Instructions to Bidders, for further review and evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC to submit a
waiver from the NFA. In the absence of a waiver, submit a justification why the
procurement of rice was made from SCMC, in spite of deficiencies in the
documentary requirements which resulted in foregone savings of
£6,300,000.00.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City ENRO to:

a) submit relevant documents that would justify the increase
of the City's solid waste contract with NLC Garbage
Services for further evaluation; and

b) formulate and study ways/means to reduce the cost of
garbage hauling services that takes up substantial amount
of the local budget so that more constituents can reap the
benefits from government-funded services.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City ENRO to
strictly comply with the regulations under RA No. 9003 and implement
programs on SWM education specifically related to proper waste
segregation, to ensure protection of the environment and compliance with the
law.

Likewise, establish government-owned MRFs in the barangay level as
required under RA No. 9003 and encourage all the Barangay SWM
Committees to promote and support the SWM programs of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) GSO personnel to include the motorcycles issued to the PNP in the
insurable properties of the City, otherwise, execute a donation to the PNP
for the latter to record in its books and to insure the vehicles; and

b) concerned GSO and Accounting personnel to provide insurance
coverage for all government buildings in the General and Special
Education Funds to guarantee indemnification in case of
occurrence of catastrophic perils.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

« require the BAC, BAC Secretariat and the BAC TWG to submit all the
lacking documents noted to support the propriety of the procurement
made by the City Government;

- « direct the City Health Officer to ensure that all drugs and medicines to
be procured are accompanied with certification that the products being
requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I,
current edition, and included in the PNDF, otherwise, a written request
with justification should be submitted to the Head of the National
Policy Staff for approval of the purchase to avoid disallowance in audit;

« instruct the BAC Secretariat to post all the scheduled biddings in
conspicuous places in the City, in addition to postings in the City website
=~ and in PhilGEPS, to inform the prospective Suppliers/Contractors of the
scheduled bidding, and attach a certification in the voucher that posting
was made in conspicuous places; and

 direct the BAC to verify and validate all documents submitted by the
Suppliers/Contractors before declaring a winning bidder.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the monthly trial Balances and transaction documents for CY 2015 and thereafter submit them promptly/regularly together with the LGU’s transaction documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of inventory of accountable forms for sale to the asset account Merchandise Inventory (1-04-01-010).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for the accounting system of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require all concerned officers to submit the required report/list for CY 2015 and thereafter submit regularly in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial statements for December 31, 2014 and the transaction documents and to submit them promptly, in the succeeding years in compliance with Section 41(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Municipal Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant work together to account for the difference of P1,503.27 in the balance of the CIV account presented in the Consolidated Balance Sheet as of December 31, 2014 and the total of the account in the individual Balance Sheet per Fund; and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries and adjust the Cash in Vault (101) account to its correct balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Municipal Finding No. 3
observations.reason: There are still items which cannot yet be reclassified without proper documents or legal basis; concerned items existed prior to the Super Typhoon and required revision which was done for some but not all items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. hold liable the responsible officers and employees for the incurrence of bank service charges for NSF checks; b. require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to be watchful in the issuance of checks by closely monitoring the LGU’s bank balances; c. make representations with the DBP to reverse the bank service charges considering that the LGU has a significant balance in its accounts; d. transfer dormant bank accounts to an account which is income generating, if it is not contrary to the MOA for the trust fund accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the account in order to reflect the correct balance of the account Livestock Inventory and Other Supplies Inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant: d. reclassify the items recorded to the account Due from Officers and Employees (123) to the correct accounts as prescribes in COA Accounting Circular No. 2006-001 dated November 9, 2006; e. adopt strategies on how to compel the accountable officers concerned to liquidate/refund their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to: 1. maintain complete PPELC for each category of property and equipment; and 2. Property Custodian to: a. conduct the annual physical inventory of property and equipment; b. maintain and keep up to date the property cards per category of property and equipment; and c. assigned property number to each piece of property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to record the advance payments to the appropriate account, Advances to Contractors (181), and observe timely recording of subsequent transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the different Registries of Public Infrastructure accounts and to transfer all completed P.I. at year-end to said Registries, and annually prepare a Summary of all Public Infrastructures (based on the different registries) and include the data in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit the LGUs Inventory and Inspection Report and Appraisal of one of the Unserviceable Property and the required COA inspection/evaluation report on the subject property.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 14
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer submitted a Pre-Repair Inspection Report and a copy of which was attached to the payments related to the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the use and provide complete disclosures of data of the standard forms of the CKDJ, GJ and JEV prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 20

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the MDRRM Fund to the account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 23

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management compose its Municipal Board and formulate the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 25

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately refund/return the balance of the PDAF to the Bureau of the Treasury in the total amount of P596,031.69.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Municipal Finding No. 26

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance to Section 73 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 93-404 and request for relief of accountability or write-off.
observations.managementAction: Office of the CY Resident Auditor was damaged by the Super Typhoon in 2013 probably the reason why auditors were seldom, if at all seen during the first two months following that fateful day. However, report revision was done and the Damage Report was submitted on February 6, 2014 in compliance to the directive of the Acting Supervising Auditor but up to the writing of this report no revision was done.
observations.reason: Due to Typhoon Yolanda the Auditor was damaged by the Super Typhoon in 2013 which was probably the reason why auditors were seldom seen; some revision done but not all.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to establish the complete records for Land Improvements in the total amount of P12,549,956.85 and to recompute the total accumulated depreciation for the period based on the complete records of Land Improvements; further Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for the said account should be prepared and maintained. Regular updates thereon should be performed, particularly the Accumulated Depreciation, in compliance with Sections 120 and 13 of NGAS for LGUs, Volumes I and II, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 Municipal Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant establish the complete records of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts, recompute and adjust its accumulated depreciation, and prepare the PPELC for all PPE accounts and to regularly update the said records including its accumulated depreciation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 Municipal Finding Nos. 5 and 2; Accountant to implement corrective measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to establish the complete records of the Construction in Progress accounts. Henceforth, the CIPLC should be updated to provide a complete information about the account, as required under the rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 Municipal Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a Special Account in the General Fund be maintained for the 20% Development Fund as required under Chapter 6, Sections 105, 106, 107 and 110, Volume I of NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 Municipal Finding No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to implement the P3,000.00 net take home pay required for each employee as provided for Section 43 of the General Provisions of the Appropriations Act for CY 2010 also known as Republic Act No. 9970.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2010 Municipal Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the conduct of physical inventory of all properties of the municipality and the submission of the subsequent reports; require the issuance and updating of all Memorandum Receipts/Acknowledgement Receipt for all purchases of equipment, and record these procurements in the property/equipment ledger cards; and adopt an adequate internal control system to ensure accuracy and reliability of accounting and property records in order to adequately safeguard the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2009 Municipal Finding Nos. 5 and 2; implement corrective measures.
